author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
tomā chāḍi’ āmi kabhu, sanātha nā hoi prabhu,
prabhuhīna dāsa nirāśraya
āmāke nā nile sātha, kaiche tumi ha’be nātha,
dayanīya ke tomāra haya

āmādera e sambandha, vidhikṛta sunirbandha,
savidhi tomāra guṇadhāma
atoeva nivedana, śuna he madhumathana,
chāḍā⋅chāḍi nahe kona kāma

e bhaktivinoda gāya, rākha more tava pāya,
pāla’ more nā chāḍa kakhana
jabe mama pāo doṣa, kariyā ucita roṣa,
daṇḍa diyā deo śrī⋅caraṇa
en_translation:
O Hari! Leaving You, I will never be protected; a servant without a Master is shelterless. If You do not take me along, how will You be the Protector? Who else is there for You to show mercy to?

This relationship of ours is ordained and firmly established by Your divine nature. Therefore, please hear my submission, O Slayer of Madhu: separation is not desired at all.

Bhaktivinoda sings: keep me at Your feet, nourish me, and never abandon me. When You find my faults, with appropriate anger, give me punishment and bestow Your divine feet.
